target_emits.name: create_wlan_ssid
target_emits.purpose: Create the Central wlan-ssids profile — the flat SSID object merging the AOS 8 virtual_ap (forward-mode, vlan) + its ssid_prof (essid, opmode). Created LOCAL at the target scope on the AP device-function. forward-mode is the operator's target decision (runtime target_mode), not a copy of the source.
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST per source virtual_ap record.

target_emits.name: create_overlay_wlan
target_emits.purpose: Bind a tunneled / hybrid SSID to its gateway cluster(s) via the overlay-wlan object. Gated by emit_when: fires only for tunneled / hybrid target modes (skipped for bridged). gw-cluster-list (which clusters + scope-ids + tunnel-type) is supplied by the skill from detection / operator; an SSID may bind to multiple clusters.
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST when target_mode is tunneled / hybrid; suppressed for bridged.

target_meta.device_function_filtering_rule: wlan-ssids + overlay-wlan are AP constructs (the SSID is broadcast/forwarded by the AP) → CAMPUS_AP. Tunneled SSIDs reach the gateway cluster via the overlay-wlan gw-cluster-list, not by being a gateway profile.

target_scope_id_resolution.rule: Central scope_id supplied by the skill at runtime (the AP scope where the SSID is broadcast — site / device-group, derived from the AOS 8 ap-group binding).
target_scope_id_resolution.input: Source ap-group binding scope + operator selection

dependencies.depends_on: central:gateway_cluster (tunneled/hybrid SSIDs bind to a cluster via overlay-wlan gw-cluster-list); central:named_vlan (the wlan-ssids vlan-name references a Central named VLAN)

merge_rule.description: The engine source record is one virtual_ap. Its ssid_prof is looked up by name from runtime_values['ssid_profiles'] (the full ssid_prof list) in the aos8_wlan_ssid preprocessing, then essid/opmode are merged onto the virtual_ap before key_mappings run. Mirrors central:policy's runtime-supplied role_records join.

objects.notes: The SSID-as-broadcast. forward_mode (one-level wrapped; absent = tunnel default), vlan, ssid_prof ref, aaa_prof ref. The engine source record IS one virtual_ap.
essid + opmode + rates. Supplied via runtime_values['ssid_profiles'] and joined to the virtual_ap by ssid_prof.profile-name. opmode is {<mode-name>: true}; mapped to the Central opmode enum.

essid_obj.notes: {name: <essid>} literal in v1; v2 swaps for {alias, use-alias} in the dual-profile case. From ssid_prof.essid.essid.

essid.notes: Raw essid string for the overlay binding.

opmode.notes: AOS 8 ssid_prof opmode key -> Central opmode enum (table in the normalizer). bSec-*/xSec are unmapped (legacy).

forward_mode.notes: From the operator's target_mode: bridged->FORWARD_MODE_BRIDGE, tunneled->FORWARD_MODE_L2, hybrid->FORWARD_MODE_MIXED.

vlan_name.notes: Named-VLAN case -> references a Central named VLAN (central:named_vlan).

needs_overlay.notes: True for tunneled / hybrid; gates the overlay-wlan emit.

gw_cluster_list.notes: [{cluster, cluster-type, tunnel-type, cluster-redundancy-type, cluster-scope-id}] supplied via runtime_values['gw_cluster_list']. An SSID may bind to multiple clusters.

unmapped_fields.from: Inline AAA on wlan-ssids (auth-server-group / acct-server-group / default-role / pre-auth-role / mac-authentication / radius-*) sourced from the virtual_ap's aaa_prof
unmapped_fields.reason: v1 ships the SSID core (identity / forwarding / encryption / vlan / cluster binding). Bridge-mode SSIDs need their AAA inlined here (the AP terminates auth); tunnel-mode SSIDs lean on the gateway aaa-profile (central:aaa_profile) reached via the overlay. Full aaa_prof-join + inline AAA is deferred to wlan_ssid v2.

unmapped_fields.from: ssid_prof rates / wmm / he_ssid_prof / dot11k / beacon-rate; virtual_ap band-steering / dynamic-mcast-optimization / broadcast-filter / drop_mcast / wmm-traffic-management; PSK secret (wpa-passphrase / wpa-hexkey)
unmapped_fields.reason: Radio/QoS tuning + advanced VAP options deferred to v2. The PSK secret (personal opmodes) must route through PII tokenization before it lands in any write path.

The two-profile essid-alias case (same SSID bridged + tunneled across locations) is deferred to v2: it emits an essid alias + a bridge profile + a tunnel profile, all sharing the alias. v1 raises a clear error for this mode.

ignored_variants.reason: Consumer filters system/default VAPs at the record level before emit_calls.

draft_notes: v1 translation. Central wlan-ssids (forward-mode L2/MIXED/BRIDGE from the operator's target_mode) + overlay-wlan binding (tunneled/hybrid only, gated by emit_when). Created LOCAL at the AP scope (device-function CAMPUS_AP) via query params.
JOIN: source record = one virtual_ap; its ssid_prof is supplied via runtime_values['ssid_profiles'] and joined by name (like central:policy's role_records).

TARGET MODE is the operator's decision (runtime target_mode), not the source forward_mode — detection only recommends it upstream in the skill. v1: bridged / tunneled / hybrid. bridged_and_tunneled (dual-profile + essid alias) deferred to v2.
DEFERRED to v2 (flagged in unmapped_fields): inline bridge-mode AAA (aaa_prof join), radio/QoS tuning, PSK secret (needs PII tokenization), and the dual-profile essid-alias case.
DEPENDENCY: tunneled/hybrid SSIDs bind to central:gateway_cluster via the overlay gw-cluster-list; vlan-name references central:named_vlan. Run those first.

NEEDS-LIVE-VERIFY (write path): device-function CAMPUS_AP vs the SSID's actual broadcast scope; opmode enum spellings for less-common modes; the overlay gw-cluster-list cluster-type / tunnel-type values.
